title: FCoP Event Model
description: Abstraction 4 of 7 — Event. Per ADR-0018, FCoP v1.0 events are DERIVED from filesystem state changes; there is NO `EVENT-*.md` envelope type. Events are emitted by an implementation's polling watcher (or equivalent) and exposed via `Project.subscribe_events()`. v1.0 ships exactly 12 event types (8 from ADR-0018 §minimal-event-set + 4 failure-related from ADR-0019). Adding events is additive (MINOR-version) per ADR-0003. v1.0 does NOT persist event log; events are emitted at subscription time only.

eventType.description: v1.0 event vocabulary. The first 8 are from ADR-0018; the last 4 are from ADR-0019 §design-details (Failure & Recovery surface). FROZEN for v1.0 — additions go to v1.1+.

subject.description: Per ADR-0018. The artefact this event is about. Shape varies by event_type; see the per-type table in spec/archived/fcop-runtime-protocol-v1.0.md §abstraction-4 (current canonical: spec/fcop-3.0-spec.md §5 Event Layer). Additional properties are permitted for forward compatibility.

actor.description: Role code of the agent involved (per agent.schema.json).

target.description: Path or identifier of the resource the actor touched.

from.description: For ROLE_SWITCHED: previous role code or 'UNBOUND'.

to.description: For ROLE_SWITCHED: new role code or 'UNBOUND'.

occurred_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp with time component. Per ADR-0018 §design-details, only single-agent monotonic ordering is guaranteed by v1.0; cross-agent happens-before is out of scope.

source.description: Where the implementation derived this event from. Per ADR-0018, v1.0 derives events from file paths and (optionally) git refs.

path.description: Filesystem path when kind = file. Relative to workspace_dir.

git_ref.description: Git ref (commit SHA or branch) when kind = git.

watcher.description: Implementation identifier of the watcher that emitted this event. e.g. 'polling-watcher@v1.0'

metadata.description: Free-form per-implementation metadata (per ADR-0018). Implementations MAY put diagnostic info here; consumers MUST tolerate unknown keys.
